stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 4 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 36.5 % (within-trial site SD = 3.1). trial site B has 8 observations with mean 31.6 % (within-trial site SD = 7.5). trial site C has 10 observations with mean 23.9 % (within-trial site SD = 4.4). trial site D has 5 observations with mean 39.3 % (within-trial site SD = 8.9). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.6 and 18.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site B: 31.6 % (shrunk from raw 31.6). Range [31.6, 31.7] as between-trial site SD varies from 7.6 to 18.9. Pooled mean: 32.5.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 32.5 % (weighted average of 4 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=12.6: B = 0.958.
Interval [31.6, 31.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (31.6) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (32.5) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
